question_type: 2. Complex question asking to summarize language
question: summary zoning requirements for urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis,  MN to build accessory dwelling unit (ADU) for single-family home.
response:
From the excerpts you provided there are ADU standards that would apply in UN2, but the retrieved material does not show any additional UN2‑specific ADU exceptions or requirements—so I’m summarizing the city’s general ADU rules found in your excerpts.

- Internal (in‑house) ADU: generally limited to 800 sq ft (can exceed 800 sq ft only if the portion of the principal residence containing the ADU existed on Jan 1, 2015); in no case may the internal ADU floor area exceed the first‑floor area of the principal residence. The entire internal ADU must be on one level; it may not create new entrances facing the public street; any stair to an above‑ground internal ADU must be enclosed or entirely at the rear. If the ADU is not a separate dwelling under the State Building Code, an owner must occupy one dwelling unit on the lot as their primary residence. Prior to permit issuance the owner must record a covenant with Hennepin County restricting use consistent with the ADU rules (the city will release the covenant if the unit later becomes a separate dwelling under the State Building Code).

- Detached ADU: height limited to 21 feet (unless a variance). Gross floor area may not exceed 1,300 sq ft or 16% of lot area, whichever is greater, but in no case exceed 1,600 sq ft or the gross floor area of the principal residence, whichever is less. The combined footprint of the detached ADU, other detached accessory structures, and any attached vehicle‑parking structures may not exceed 800 sq ft or 10% of the lot area, whichever is greater (connected elements such as decks, overhangs, stairs count toward this). Minimum interior side yard for a detached ADU is 3 ft (no reduction where vehicle access doors face the interior side lot line); minimum rear yard may be reduced to 3 ft except where vehicle access doors face the rear lot line.

- Base size limit: 800 sq ft (may exceed 800 sq ft only if the portion of the principal structure containing the ADU existed as of Jan 1, 2015). - Entrances/stairs: Creation of the ADU must not create additional entrances facing the public street. Stairways to an internal ADU above ground floor must be enclosed or located entirely to the rear of the principal structure.
- Owner‑occupancy and covenant: If the ADU is internal and not considered a separate dwelling unit under the Minnesota State Building Code, at least one owner must occupy a dwelling unit on the zoning lot as their primary residence. Before permit issuance the owner(s) must record a covenant with Hennepin County restricting use to comply with these ADU rules; the zoning administrator will release the covenant if later the unit becomes a separate dwelling under the State Building Code.

- Footprint and other structures: Combined footprint of detached ADU plus all other detached accessory structures and any attached parking areas may not exceed 800 sq ft or 10% of lot area, whichever is greater. Connected elements (decks, stairs, overhangs >2 ft, etc.) count toward that footprint limit.
- Location and setbacks: Detached ADUs must be located entirely to the rear of the principal structure. Minimum interior side yard generally not less than 3 ft (and no reduction where vehicle access doors face the interior side lot line). question:
Aim: I am looking for relevant information and requirements from Minneapolis zoning code that I need to know as a single-family home owner who wants to build a accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on my property in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. 

Output: A summary of relevant zoning requirements for building an ADU on a single-family home in [zoning district returned by question 1] in Minneapolis, MN.
response: In UN2 (Urban Neighborhood 2) districts ADUs are permitted accessory to a permitted single‑family dwelling; the key Minneapolis Zoning Code provisions are in Article IX (550.1400–550.1460). Summary: internal, attached, and detached ADUs are allowed (550.1420) but only one ADU per zoning lot and the ADU may not create a separate tax parcel (550.1430); applications are administratively reviewed by the zoning administrator (550.1410). General development controls include limits on balconies/decks (not facing interior side yards; decks/rooftop decks ≥5 ft from interior side property lines) (550.1430). Internal ADUs: generally limited to 800 sq ft, must be on a single level, may not add new street‑facing entrances, stairways to above‑ground internal ADUs must be enclosed or at the rear, and owner‑occupancy and a recorded covenant are required if the ADU is not a separate dwelling under the State Building Code (550.1440; covenant recording details in Context 1). Attached ADUs: max 800 sq ft, may not add new street‑facing entrances, primary exterior materials must match the principal structure, and stairway placement rules apply (550.1450). Detached ADUs: height generally ≤21 ft (unless variance), gross floor area limits of 1,300 sq ft or 16% of lot area (whichever is greater) but never exceeding 1,600 sq ft or the principal structure’s GFA (550.1460). Covenants run with the land and must be recorded with Hennepin County before a permit is issued; the zoning administrator can record a release after inspection if the ADU is removed (550.1440 and related covenant provisions).

Owner‑occupancy covenants are required in certain situations: property owners must record a covenant with Hennepin County (in a form prescribed by the zoning administrator) restricting use/owner‑occupancy when the ADU is not a separate dwelling unit under the Minnesota State Building Code; the recorded covenant must be returned to the zoning administrator before a building permit is issued, and a release may be recorded if the ADU later meets MSBC dwelling‑unit requirements. ADU applications are administratively reviewed by the zoning administrator (file on the required form; decisions final subject to appeal to the Board of Adjustment).

question_type: 2. Complex question asking to summarize language
question: I am a single-family home owner and I want to build a accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on my property in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. What are the zoning requirements, restrictions, or permits I would need to keep in mind for my zoning district?
response:
Based on the retrieved context, a single-family home located at **2712 Colfax Ave South, Minneapolis, MN 55408** is zoned for a State Credentialed Care Facility, serving six (6) or fewer persons. Additionally, the property is located in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, which has the following requirements:

- Accessory dwelling units are allowed, but must be detached from the principal residential structure and not exceed 21 feet in height.
- Open parking spaces must be at least 3 feet away from the habitable space of a dwelling.
- Detached parking garages serving residential uses and detached accessory dwelling units must be located entirely to the rear of the principal residential structure.
- Firearms dealers existing on or before October 7, 1995, and in all other respects in conformance with the provisions of this ordinance, shall be permitted to continue as nonconforming uses in accordance with the provisions of Article VI, Nonconforming Uses and Structures.

The property owner(s) must also comply with the requirements of the attached covenant, which states that the owner(s) agree to restrict use of the principal and accessory dwelling units in compliance with the requirements of this section and notify all prospective buyers.

Therefore, the user can expect the following:

- A State Credentialed Care Facility, serving six (6) or fewer persons, is located on the property.
- The property is located in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, which has specific requirements for accessory dwelling units and detached parking garages.
- The property owner(s) must comply with the attached covenant and may be subject to nonconforming uses and structures.

- lot area: 1000 sq feet
- height of building: 20 feet
-  number of dwellings in the building: 5
-   side and rear yard setback: 5 feet
response:
Based on the retrieved context, the proposed property specifications meet the single-family zoning requirements for Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. Here's a summary of the requirements:

- Lot area: 1000 sq feet, which is within the allowed maximum lot area for an accessory dwelling unit in UN2 zoning district.
- Height of building: 20 feet, which is within the allowed height limit for an accessory dwelling unit in UN2 zoning district.
- Number of dwellings in the building: 5, which is within the allowed maximum number of dwellings for an accessory dwelling unit in UN2 zoning district.
- Side and rear yard setback: 5 feet, which is within the allowed setback requirements for an accessory dwelling unit in UN2 zoning district.
- Enclosed storage: The proposed property meets the requirements for enclosed storage, which is a separate dwelling unit that provides an enclosed storage area not less than 200 square feet in area.
- Alternative compliance: The proposed property complies with the alternative compliance requirements for accessory dwelling units in UN2 zoning district, which includes providing access to the outside of the building without using stairs or elevators.
- Lot controls: The proposed property complies with the lot controls for accessory dwelling units in UN2 zoning district, which includes not combining land, lots, parcels, or tracts.
- Development plan: The proposed development plan meets the requirements of Article V, Site Plan Review Standards in UN2 zoning district, including providing a site plan design standard for single-, two-, and three-family dwellings.
- Site plan consistency: The proposed development plan meets the requirements of Article V, General Standards for Residential Uses in UN2 zoning district, including providing a minimum window requirement for walls facing a public street or sidewalk.

Therefore, the user can expect that the proposed property specifications meet the single-family zoning requirements for Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN.

The UN3 Urban Neighborhood District allows predominantly moderate to large-scale residential uses near transit routes and METRO stations.

* Required street frontage: Each zoning lot shall have frontage on a public street.
* Private easement: A private easement may be used to satisfy the requirement of public street frontage, subject to certain standards.

Overall, the UN3 Urban Neighborhood District is designed to provide a balance between residential and commercial uses, with a focus on promoting employment opportunities and serving the local market.

question_type: 2. Complex question asking to summarize language
question: I am a single-family home owner and I want to build a accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on my property in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. What are the zoning requirements, restrictions, or permits I would need to keep in mind for my zoning district?
response:
To build an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on your property in Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN, you would need to comply with the following zoning requirements and restrictions:

1. **Residential Structure or Property Zoned As:** Single-Family Dwelling
2. **Zoning Requirements:** The ADU must be built on the same zoning lot as the principal residential structure, and the owner(s) must occupy at least one dwelling unit on the zoning lot as their primary place of residence.
3. **Covenant:** You must file a covenant with the City of Minneapolis to restrict use of the principal and ADU units in compliance with the requirements of this section.
4. **Lot Size and Floor Area:** The ADU must be built on a lot of no more than 7,500 square feet in area, with a gross floor area not exceeding 4,000 square feet.
5. **Side Yard Requirements:** The ADU's side yard must be located behind the principal structure and meet the applicable side yard requirements of the adjacent district.
6. **Window Clearance:** The ADU's side yard must not be obstructed by a window facing the shared lot line, unless the window is within 10 feet of the side lot line and serving a residential use on the adjacent property.
7. **Parking:** The ADU must not have a parking garage or other parking structure on the same lot, except for a single vehicle parked in an enclosed structure.
8. **Storage:** The ADU must provide sufficient storage for each dwelling unit or rooming unit, with separate and distinct storage areas from the habitable area of the units.
9. **Height and Building Form:** The ADU must not exceed 64 feet in height, with a minimum of 5 feet additional height for buildings 64 feet or taller.
10. **Accessory Retail Sales:** The ADU's sales activities must comply with the use standards for farmstands specified in Article III, Accessory Uses and Structures in this chapter.
11. **Market Gardens:** The ADU must be located on a rooftop or indoor operation, unless it is accessory to a principal use located on the same zoning lot.
12. **Animals:** Keeping of animals is prohibited.
13. **Outdoor Growing:** Outdoor growing associated with the ADU must be exempt from the enclosed building requirements of the zoning ordinance, except for cannabis cultivation, which must not be conducted in greenhouses, hoop houses, tents, or similar structures.

question:

response:
**Summary:**

For the property located at 2817 Pleasant Ave, Minneapolis, MN 55408, which is a multi-family home in an urban neighborhood zoned as an Urban Neighborhood (UN3) and a residential mixed-use district, we can determine the zoning district and built form overlay district as follows:

**Zoning District:**

The Urban Neighborhood (UN3) zoning district is established in the Minneapolis, MN zoning code (530.100. - General purpose. The urban neighborhood districts are established to allow residential uses and small-scale institutional and civic uses. Select commercial uses are allowed through the adaptive reuse of existing structures. Urban neighborhood districts are primarily distinguished by the types of residential uses allowed.)

**Built Form Overlay District:**

The Downtown Housing Overlay District (DH Housing Overlay District) is established in the Minneapolis, MN zoning code (535.400. - Purpose. The DH Downtown Housing Overlay District is established to provide areas that offer affordable housing that may not meet the regulations of the primary zoning district, including minimum spacing requirements for congregate living residential uses.) The DH Housing Overlay District is a built form overlay district that is established to provide areas that offer affordable housing near transit routes and METRO stations.

**Summary of Overlay District Guidelines:**

The built form overlay district designation for 2817 Pleasant Ave is the Downtown Housing Overlay District (DH Housing Overlay District). This district is established to provide areas that offer affordable housing near transit routes and METRO stations. The guidelines for the DH Housing Overlay District are outlined in the Minneapolis, MN zoning code (535.400. - Purpose. The DH Downtown Housing Overlay District is established to provide areas that offer affordable housing that may not meet the regulations of the primary zoning district, including minimum spacing requirements for congregate living residential uses. The guidelines for the DH Housing Overlay District are as follows:

* Minimum spacing requirements for congregate living residential uses are not applicable.
* The maximum floor area ratio requirements are as set forth in Table 540-2, Maximum Floor Area Ratio.
* The maximum floor area ratio may not be achievable in districts with lot coverage and impervious surface regulations.
* The minimum floor area ratio requirements are as set forth in Table 540-3, Maximum Floor Area Ratio for Cluster Developments.
* The maximum floor area ratio may be increased as authorized in each built form overlay district for that portion of development in which the development is located.
* The minimum height requirements are as set forth in Table 540-3, Maximum Floor Area Ratio for Cluster Developments.
* The maximum lot coverage requirements are as set forth in Table 540-2, Maximum Floor Area Ratio.

**Additional Considerations:**

The built form overlay district designation for 2817 Pleasant Ave requires the property to be subject to the provisions of both the primary zoning district and the overlay district. The overlay district regulations may be more or less restrictive than the primary zoning district, and where the provisions of the overlay and primary zoning districts are in conflict, the provisions of the overlay district shall govern.

response:
The **Urban Neighborhood 3 (UN3)** zoning district in Minneapolis, MN, is designed to accommodate residential uses with specific regulations for density, lot sizes, and permitted uses. Key provisions for the UN3 district include:

1. **Permitted Uses**: The district allows for single-family, two-family, and three-family dwellings, as well as community centers, educational facilities, parks, and recreational facilities. For example, community centers require a minimum lot size of 5,000 square feet, while educational facilities require 10,000 square feet.

2. **Density and Floor Area Ratio (FAR)**: The UN3 district has specific FAR requirements. For instance, single-family dwellings have an FAR of 0.5, two-family dwellings have an FAR of 0.6, and three-family dwellings have an FAR of 0.7. Other uses have an FAR of 1.4.

3. **Lot Requirements**: Residential buildings with four or more dwelling units are permitted on lots of record, provided they meet yard dimensions and other district requirements (excluding lot area or width). Nonconforming lots regarding maximum lot area retain the rights of conforming lots but cannot be enlarged except as allowed by the Built Form Overlay District regulations.

4. **Compliance**: All uses must comply with general performance standards, landscaping, screening, and curbing requirements applicable to the district.

5. **Overlay Districts**: The property may also be subject to additional regulations if located within a Built Form Overlay District or other overlay districts, such as the Shoreland Overlay District.

This summary provides an overview of the UN3 zoning district's regulations, focusing on residential uses, density, and compliance requirements.

question: zoning requirement checklist for multi-family home construction in urban neighborhood 3 (UN3) zoning district Minneapolis, MN
response:
Based on the provided context, here is a zoning requirement checklist for constructing a multi-family home in the **Urban Neighborhood 3 (UN3)** zoning district in Minneapolis, MN:

### Zoning Requirement Checklist for Multi-Family Home Construction in UN3 District

1. **Lot Requirements**:
   - **Lot of Record**: The residential building with four or more dwelling units must be on a lot of record existing on the effective date of the ordinance.
   - **Yard Dimensions**: Ensure that yard dimensions and all other requirements for the district are met, excluding lot area or lot width.
   - **Lot Merger**: If the lot is nonconforming as to lot width or lot area and is located within the SH Shoreland Overlay District, it must be combined with adjacent lots under the same ownership to meet the full lot width and lot area requirements.
   - **Maximum Lot Area**: Lots nonconforming as to maximum lot area shall have all the rights of a conforming lot and shall not be enlarged except as allowed in Chapter 540, Built Form Overlay Districts, Article VIII, Lot Dimensions

2. **Floor Area Ratio (FAR)**:
   - **FAR for Multi-Family Dwellings**: The FAR for multi-family dwellings is 1.4. Ensure the proposed building adheres to this ratio.

3. **Outdoor Children's Play Area**:
   - **Requirement**: If the development includes at least one dwelling with three or more bedrooms, an outdoor children's play area must be provided.
   - **Criteria**: The lot area must be at least 20,000 square feet, located in the UN3 district, at least 1,500 feet from a public park, and outside the UA University Area Overlay District.
   - **Play Area Size**: Minimum of 50 square feet for each unit containing three or more bedrooms, with a minimum of 300 square feet and a maximum of 2,000 square feet.
   - **Design**: The play area must be secure, separated from parking and maneuvering areas, and designed to facilitate adult supervision.

4. **Affordable Housing**:
   - **Inclusionary Housing**: Comply with the city's inclusionary housing requirements by providing affordable units on-site.
   - **Unit Requirement**: The development must include at least 20 dwelling units to qualify for the affordable housing bonus.
   - **Premium Value**: The premium value shall be double if the affordable units are on-site and affordable at or below 30% of the area median income.

5. **Child Care Center** (if included):
   - **Space Requirement**: Not less than 2,000 square feet of enclosed space.
   - **Compliance**: Meet the specific use standards for child care centers in Chapter 545, Use Regulations.

6. **Construction Type**:
   - **Building Code**: Not less than 60% of the floor area of the development must be within structures classified as Type IA, Type IB, Type IIA, or Type IV by the building code.

7. **Enclosed Parking**:
   - **Location**: All off-street parking must be within the building, entirely below grade, or in a parking garage of at least two levels.
   - **Drive Aisles**: All drive aisles must be within the structure, except in the Interior 3 District, where drive aisles and vehicle maneuvering areas may be outdoors.
   - **Electric Vehicle Charging**: Provide electric vehicle charging infrastructure as per section 555.320.

8. **Environmental Sustainability**:
   - **Climate Resiliency**: Achieve at least one of the following standards:
     - Any performance standard (LEED, PHIUS, EGC, etc.) that meets the Minnesota Sustainable Building 2030 (SB 2030) 2010-2014 Energy Standard.
     - Derive not less than 40% of electricity usage from renewable energy.

9. **Compliance with Standards**:
   - **General Performance Standards**: Maintain compliance with all general performance standards, landscaping, screening, and curbing requirements.
   - **Feasibility**: The zoning administrator may require compliance with a portion of applicable standards if strict compliance is practically or economically infeasible.

10. **Site Plan Review**:
    - **Design Standards**: Comply with applicable regulations, including front entrance, window area, and walkway requirements.
    - **Points System**: Obtain the minimum number of required site plan review points from Table 550-2, Single-, Two-, and Three-Family Dwellings.

This checklist summarizes the key zoning requirements for constructing a multi-family home in the UN3 district based on the provided context. Always consult the full zoning ordinance and local authorities for complete and detailed requirements.

Based on the retrieved context, here is a summary of the zoning requirements for building an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district in Minneapolis, MN:

- **Size and Height**: A detached ADU cannot exceed 21 feet in height. The gross floor area of a detached ADU cannot exceed 1,300 square feet or 16% of the lot area, whichever is greater, but it cannot exceed 1,600 square feet or the gross floor area of the principal residential structure, whichever is less. The combined footprint of a detached ADU and all other detached accessory structures cannot exceed 800 square feet or 10% of the lot area, whichever is greater.

- **Yard Requirements**: The minimum interior side yard requirement for a detached ADU is 3 feet, except where vehicle access doors face the interior side lot line, in which case no reduction of the required yard is permitted. The minimum rear yard requirement may be reduced to 3 feet, except where vehicle access doors face the rear lot line.

- **Internal ADUs**: An internal ADU (within the principal residential structure) can exceed 800 square feet only if the portion of the structure in which the ADU is located was in existence as of January 1, 2015. The ADU must be located on one level and cannot result in additional entrances facing the public street. Stairways leading to an internal ADU above the ground floor must be enclosed or located entirely to the rear of the principal residential structure.

- **Owner Occupancy**: The property owner must occupy at least one dwelling unit on the zoning lot as their primary place of residence. If the owner cannot fulfill this requirement, the ADU features must be removed.

- **Covenant**: Prior to issuance of a permit for an ADU, the owner must file a covenant with the Hennepin County recorder stating that they agree to comply with the ADU requirements.

These regulations ensure that ADUs are integrated into the residential character of the neighborhood while providing additional housing options.

14. **Compliance with Building Code**: Ensure that the project meets all relevant requirements in the building code, as referenced in the context.

response:

1. **Size and Dimensions**:
   - An ADU can exceed 800 square feet only if the portion of the principal residential structure where the ADU is located was in existence as of January 1, 2015.
   - The floor area of an internal ADU cannot exceed the floor area of the first floor of the principal residential structure and must be located on one level.
   - For detached ADUs, the gross floor area cannot exceed 1,300 square feet or 16% of the lot area, whichever is greater, but in no case can it exceed 1,600 square feet or the gross floor area of the principal residential structure, whichever is less.
   - The combined footprint of a detached ADU and other detached accessory structures cannot exceed 800 square feet or 10% of the lot area, whichever is greater.

2. **Placement and Yard Requirements**:
   - Detached ADUs must be located entirely to the rear of the principal residential structure.
   - The minimum interior side yard requirement for a detached ADU is 3 feet, except where vehicle access doors face the interior side lot line, in which case no reduction of the required yard is permitted.
   - The minimum rear yard requirement for a detached ADU may be reduced to 3 feet, except where vehicle access doors face the rear lot line, in which case no reduction of the required yard is permitted on the side with the vehicle access doors.

3. **Parking and Entrances**:
   - The creation of an ADU cannot result in additional entrances facing the public street on the principal residential structure.
   - Stairways leading to an internal ADU located above the ground floor must be enclosed or located entirely to the rear of the principal residential structure.

4. **Owner Occupancy**:
   - The property owner must occupy at least one dwelling unit on the zoning lot as their primary place of residence if the ADU is internal to the principal residential structure and not a separate dwelling unit under the Minnesota State Building Code.

5. **Permits and Covenants**:
   - Prior to issuance of a permit for an ADU, the owner must file a covenant with the Hennepin County recorder stating that they agree to restrict use of the principal and accessory dwelling units in compliance with the zoning requirements.

6. **Height Limit**:
   - A detached ADU cannot exceed 21 feet in height, except as authorized by variance.

7. **Distance from Dwelling**:
   - A detached accessory structure must not be located closer than 6 feet from the habitable space of a dwelling.

question: I am a single-family home owner and I want to build a accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on my property in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. What are the zoning requirements, restrictions, or permits I would need to keep in mind for my zoning district?
response:
Based on the retrieved context, here are the key zoning requirements, restrictions, and permit details to consider when building an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) in the **Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2)** zoning district in Minneapolis, MN:

1. **Permitted ADUs**: Internal, attached, and detached ADUs are allowed, but only one ADU is permitted per zoning lot. The principal residential structure must be a single-family or two-family dwelling.

2. **Size and Design Standards**:
   - **Attached ADUs**: Maximum gross floor area of 800 square feet. No additional entrances facing the public street are allowed. Stairways leading to the ADU must be enclosed or located at the rear. Primary exterior materials must match the principal structure.
   - **Detached ADUs**: Maximum height of 21 feet. Gross floor area cannot exceed 1,300 square feet or 16% of the lot area, whichever is greater, but cannot exceed 1,600 square feet or the gross floor area of the principal structure.
   - **Internal ADUs**: Limited to 800 square feet, must be on one level, and cannot result in additional street-facing entrances.

3. **Owner Occupancy**: If the ADU is internal to the principal structure and not a separate dwelling unit under Minnesota State Building Code, the owner must occupy one dwelling unit on the property as their primary residence.

4. **Permits and Covenants**: Prior to issuing a permit, the owner must file a covenant with Hennepin County agreeing to comply with ADU requirements. This covenant runs with the land and must be returned to the zoning administrator before the building permit is issued.

5. **Review Process**: The zoning administrator conducts the administrative review of ADU applications, and their decisions are final unless appealed to the board of adjustment.

6. **Additional Requirements**: Balconies cannot face interior side yards, decks must be at least 5 feet from interior side property lines, and ADUs cannot create separate tax parcels.

If you plan to proceed, ensure you comply with all these requirements and consult the Minneapolis Zoning Code for any additional details or updates.

This district supports higher-density residential development, including multifamily housing, and allows for a mix of institutional and civic uses. Key guidelines for building in this district include:

1. **Allowed Uses**: Predominantly residential, with provisions for institutional and civic uses. Select commercial uses may be permitted through adaptive reuse of existing structures ( Context 2).

2. **Built Form Overlay Districts**: Developments must comply with the regulations of the applicable built form overlay district, which may include Interior, Corridor, Transit, or Parks overlays. These overlays dictate standards for lot dimensions, floor area ratio (FAR), height, and lot coverage (Context 1, Context 3).

3. **Lot Dimensions**: The more restrictive requirements (minimum and maximum) for lot area and lot width between the primary zoning and built form overlay districts will apply to the entire development ( Context 1).

4. **Floor Area Ratio (FAR)**: The more restrictive FAR requirements of the built form overlay districts will apply. Minimum FAR requirements, if applicable, will also be enforced (Context 1).

5. **Height**: Maximum and minimum height requirements are determined by the built form overlay district. Height increases are only allowed as authorized within the specific overlay district ( Context 1).

6. **Lot Coverage**: Maximum lot coverage requirements are dictated by the built form overlay district (Context 1).

7. **Institutional and Civic Uses**: UN3 allows for larger institutional and civic facilities, such as educational facilities, community centers, and religious places of assembly, with specific size limits (e.g., 20,000 sq. ft. for educational facilities) (Context 5).

8. **Zoning Compliance**: All developments must adhere to the primary zoning district regulations and the additional provisions of the built form overlay district (Context 1, Context 2).

**References**:

3. **Owner Occupancy**: The property owner must occupy at least one dwelling unit on the lot as their primary residence if the ADU is internal to the principal structure (Context 4).

4. **Permitting and Covenants**: Before establishing an ADU, the owner must file a covenant with the Hennepin County recorder, agreeing to comply with the zoning requirements (Context 4). The fee for an administrative application for an ADU is $325 (Context 6).

5. **Zoning District Compliance**: The primary zoning and built form overlay district regulations, including use, building bulk, lot area, parking, and yard requirements, apply to the entire lot (Context 5).

These requirements ensure that ADUs are integrated into the urban neighborhood in a way that aligns with the character and standards of the district.

1. **Permitted ADUs**: Internal, attached, and detached ADUs are allowed for single-family or two-family dwellings. Only one ADU is permitted per zoning lot, and it cannot create a separate tax parcel.

2. **Size Restrictions**:
   - Attached or internal ADUs are limited to **800 square feet** of gross floor area.
   - Internal ADUs must be located on one level and cannot result in additional street-facing entrances.
   - Attached ADUs cannot have balconies facing an interior side yard, and decks must be at least **5 feet** from an interior side property line.

3. **Owner Occupancy**: If the ADU is internal to the principal residential structure, the property owner must occupy one of the dwelling units on the lot as their primary residence. If this requirement is not met, the ADU features must be removed.

4. **Covenant Requirement**: Prior to obtaining a permit for an ADU, the property owner must file a covenant with Hennepin County stating that they agree to comply with the ADU requirements. This covenant runs with the land and is binding on future owners.

5. **Review Process**: ADU applications are reviewed administratively by the zoning administrator, and their decisions are final unless appealed.

For more detailed information or clarification, consult the Minneapolis Zoning Code or contact the Minneapolis Planning and Zoning Department.

response:
Based on the retrieved context from the Minneapolis zoning code, here is a summary of relevant zoning requirements for building an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) on a single-family home in the **urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district** in Minneapolis, MN:

1. **Types of ADUs Allowed**: Internal, attached, and detached ADUs are permitted, provided the principal residential structure is a single-family or two-family dwelling. ADUs are not allowed for other uses.

2. **Number of ADUs**: Only one ADU is allowed per zoning lot.

3. **Size and Dimensions**:
   - **Internal or Attached ADUs**: Maximum gross floor area of 800 square feet. If the ADU is internal and was part of the structure as of January 1, 2015, it may exceed 800 square feet but cannot exceed the first-floor area of the principal structure.
   - **Detached ADUs**: Maximum gross floor area of 1,300 square feet or 16% of the lot area (whichever is greater), but not exceeding 1,600 square feet or the floor area of the principal structure (whichever is less). Height limit is 21 feet unless a variance is granted.
   - **Decks and Balconies**: Decks must be at least 5 feet from interior side property lines, and balconies cannot face an interior side yard.

4. **Entrances**: Additional entrances facing the public street are not allowed for attached or internal ADUs.

5. **Owner-Occupancy**: For internal ADUs that are not separate dwelling units under the Minnesota State Building Code, the property owner must occupy at least one dwelling unit on the lot as their primary residence. This does not apply to detached ADUs unless established before March 6, 2021.

6. **Covenant Requirement**: A covenant must be filed with Hennepin County to ensure compliance with ADU regulations. This covenant runs with the land and must be adhered to by all future property owners.

7. **Design Standards**: The primary exterior materials of attached or detached ADUs must match those of the principal structure. Detached ADUs must include windows covering at least 5% of the facade facing an alley or public street.

8. **Setback Requirements**: Detached ADUs must be at least 10 feet from the habitable portion of the principal structure and comply with specific corner side lot line and rear lot line setbacks.

9. **Application Process**: An application for an ADU must be filed with the zoning administrator, who will conduct an administrative review. Decisions by the zoning administrator are final but can be appealed to the board of adjustment.

These requirements are specific to the UN2 zoning district and are intended to encourage innovation in housing design while maintaining the character of the neighborhood.

question:
Aim: I am looking for relevant information and requirements from Minneapolis zoning code that I need to know as a single-family home owner who wants to build a accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on my property in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis, MN. 

Output: A summary of relevant zoning requirements for building an ADU on a single-family home in [zoning district returned by question 1] in Minneapolis, MN.
response:
Based on the retrieved context from the Minneapolis zoning code, here is a summary of relevant zoning requirements for building an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) on a single-family home in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district:

1. **Owner Occupancy**: The property owner must occupy at least one of the dwelling units (either the primary residence or the ADU) as their primary place of residence. If this requirement is not met, the ADU must be removed or modified to comply. A covenant must be filed with the Hennepin County recorder to enforce this requirement.

2. **ADU Size and Placement**:
   - **Attached ADU**: The maximum gross floor area for an attached ADU is 800 square feet. Stairways leading to an internal ADU above the ground floor must be enclosed or located entirely to the rear of the principal structure. The primary exterior materials of the ADU must match those of the principal residence.
   - **Detached ADU**: A detached ADU cannot exceed 21 feet in height. The maximum floor area for detached accessory structures, including ADUs, is 800 square feet or 10% of the lot area, whichever is greater.

3. **Entrances**: The creation of an ADU shall not result in additional entrances facing the public street on the principal residential structure.

4. **Parking**: If the ADU is part of a cluster development, the gross floor area of all principal structures containing one to three dwelling units shall not exceed 4,000 square feet.

5. **Compliance with Building Code**: ADUs must comply with the Minnesota State Building Code and relevant zoning regulations, including those specified in Chapter 550, Article IX, Accessory Dwelling Unit Standards.

6. **Site Plan Review**: New construction must comply with the applicable requirements of Article V, Site Plan Review Standards, and residential design standards.

For more detailed regulations, refer to the specific sections of the Minneapolis zoning code, including **Chapter 550, Article IX**, and relevant tables such as **Table 540-17**.

question:
Aim: I am trying to fill a permit application for constructing a small multi-family building in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district,  Minneapolis, MN. Provide a checklist of all information and documentation I need to fill the permit application successfully per the zoning ordinance.

Output: A checklist of requested information stated in the Aim.
response:
Based on the retrieved context from the Minneapolis zoning code, here is a checklist of information and documentation required to successfully fill out a permit application for constructing a small multi-family building in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district:

### Checklist for Permit Application

1. **Completed Application Form**:
   - Obtain and fill out the application form approved by the zoning administrator.

2. **Pre-Application Meeting**:
   - Schedule and attend a pre-application meeting with city staff to review application procedures, requirements, and applicable zoning ordinance provisions.

3. **Site Plan**:
   - Provide a dimensioned or annotated plan accurately showing the proposed building or structure, including:
     - Building bulk, height, and use.
     - Existing buildings and uses in relation to lot lines.
     - Description of adjoining land uses.

4. **Supporting Information**:
   - Include all required supporting information as specified by city ordinance, the zoning administrator, or the application form. This may include:
     - A description of the proposal and its comparison to nearby land uses.
     - Any potential environmental hazards due to existing or proposed land uses.
     - An air quality plan, dust management plan, sound attenuation plan, drainage plan, landscape plan, and traffic plan if applicable.

5. **Fees**:
   - Pay all required fees as listed in **Table 525-1, Fees**.

6. **Environmental Reviews**:
   - Complete and submit all required environmental reviews.

7. **State and Federal Permits**:
   - Obtain and submit all required state and federal permits if the property is located in the FP Floodplain Overlay District.

8. **Neighborhood Notification**:
   - Provide evidence that notification of the application has been mailed or delivered to the ward council office and neighborhood group(s) for the area. Include:
     - A description of the project.
     - The zoning approvals needed for the project.
     - The address of the property for which zoning approval is sought.

9. **Phased Development**:
   - If the project includes phased development, ensure all phases conform to applicable zoning regulations.

10. **Site Plan Review**:
    - If the proposal includes four (4) or more new or additional dwelling units, ensure the site plan review application can be reviewed administratively if it meets the criteria (e.g., fewer than 20 new or additional dwelling units).

11. **Compliance with Zoning Ordinance**:
    - Ensure the proposal complies with all applicable provisions of the zoning ordinance, including height, setback, and use regulations.

12. **Additional Documentation**:
    - Include any other documentation or information required by the zoning administrator to determine zoning compliance.

By following this checklist and ensuring all required information and documentation are submitted, you can successfully fill out the permit application for constructing a small multi-family building in the UN2 zoning district.

question: summarize zoning ordinance for urban neighborhood 3 (UN3) zoning district in Minneapolis, MN
response:
Here is a concise summary of the UN3 Urban Neighborhood District according to the Minneapolis Code of Ordinances.

UN3 District – Summary (Urban Neighborhood 3)

The UN3 Urban Neighborhood District is intended for moderate- to large-scale residential development, especially near transit routes and METRO stations. It allows more intensive residential uses than UN1 and UN2, supporting higher-density housing forms.

Allowed Uses

UN3 allows predominantly residential uses across a broad scale, including higher-density dwellings. Small-scale institutional and civic uses are also allowed, as in all Urban Neighborhood districts. Commercial uses are limited and generally require adaptive reuse of existing buildings unless granted through a Planned Unit Development (PUD).

Site & Development Standards

Development in UN3 must also comply with the applicable Built Form Overlay District, which controls height, floor area ratio (FAR), impervious surface limits, and yard requirements. All property is regulated by both the primary zoning district (UN3) and the applicable built form overlay district.

Yards & Setbacks

Standard minimum setbacks for all Urban Neighborhood districts (including UN3) include:

Front yard: typically 20 ft for Interior built form districts; 15 ft in most Corridor/Transit built form categories.

Interior side & rear yards: vary by building height (5 ft for buildings <42 ft; increasing with height).

Parking & Access

UN3 has limited parking requirements, and off-site parking is only allowed in specific compatible districts (e.g., RM districts, CM districts, downtown districts). Driveway widths are restricted to 10–20 ft in Urban Neighborhood districts.

Nonconforming Lots

UN3 allows construction of multi-unit residential buildings on existing small lots even when lot area or lot width is nonconforming, as long as all other district requirements (yards, height, etc.) are met.

If you want, I can also summarize the built form overlay district that applies to your site once you provide it (e.g., Interior 3, Corridor 4, Transit 10, etc.).

Below is a code-based, ordinance-grounded summary of everything the Minneapolis zoning code provides about building in the Urban Neighborhood 3 (UN3) Zoning District, based solely on the Minneapolis_MN_Code_of_Ordinances.pdf.
Because the code does not list parcel-specific built form overlays, this summary includes all generally applicable UN-district + built-form rules found in the retrieved sections.

UN3 Urban Neighborhood District — Summary for Development
Purpose of UN3

The UN3 district is defined as allowing “predominantly moderate to large-scale residential uses near transit routes and METRO stations.”
Source: 530.130.

Allowed Uses (General UN District Framework)

Urban Neighborhood Districts (UN1–UN3) permit:

Residential uses (1–3 family, multifamily).

Select institutional/civic uses.

Limited adaptive reuse commercial uses.

This framework is defined in 530.100, which applies to all UN districts: urban neighborhoods allow residential and “small scale institutional and civic uses,” with certain commercial uses allowed only through adaptive reuse.

Built Form Requirements Applicable to UN3

Because every parcel is also assigned a built form overlay district, and the code requires compliance with both the primary zoning district and built form overlay (540-series standards), the following rules apply to all UN districts, including UN3.

1. Interior Side & Rear Yards

Minimums are set by Table 540-20, which applies to all urban neighborhood districts:

Building Height (ft)	Min. Interior Side & Rear Yard (ft)
< 42	5
42–52.99	7
53–63.99	9
64–74.99	11
75–85.99	13
86–96.99	15
97–107.99	17
108–119.99	19
≥120	20

Source: Table 540-20; repeated in 540.870.

Increased Side Yard Conditions

Additional side-yard increases apply when:

Building along the side lot line exceeds 75% of lot depth → +2 ft required.

If adjacent to BFC6 or transit built form district while the site is in BFI1/BFI2 → +5 ft required for buildings ≥64 ft.

Also, if a residential principal entrance faces the side lot line, the minimum side yard must be ≥15 ft unless the district otherwise requires more.

2. Corner Side Yards (Urban Neighborhood Districts)

Minimums depend on height (Table 540-19):

<42 ft → 8 ft

42–52.99 ft → 10 ft

53–63.99 ft → 12 ft

64–74.99 ft → 14 ft

≥75 ft → 15 ft

Source: Table 540-19.

Reductions may apply where adjacent to goods & services corridors or certain transit overlay districts.

3. Driveways & Parking Requirements
Driveways (All UN Districts)

Driveway obstructions and placement rules are provided in 555.510:

Driveways allowed in required front and side yards for residential uses if leading to properly located parking areas.

Non-residential uses have stricter limits (e.g., 5 ft from residential lot lines).

Off-Site Parking Allowed for Uses in UN3

Table 555-5 lists permitted off-site parking locations:

A use located in UN3 may place off-site parking only in:

UN3

RM1, RM2, RM3

CM1–CM4

DT1–DT2

Source: Table 555-5.

4. Minimum Lot Area Requirements (Selected Uses)

Minimum lot sizes apply to certain uses within UN3, such as:

Parks & open spaces: 20,000 sq ft in UN districts.

Recreational facilities: 20,000 sq ft in UN districts.

Social & cultural assembly: 20,000 sq ft in UN districts.

Convents/monasteries/religious assembly: 10,000 sq ft minimum in UN districts.

These appear in the larger Table 540-17–related section.

5. Built Form Overlay Requirement

All parcels must comply with both a primary zoning district (UN3) and a built form overlay district:

“All property in the city shall be located within a primary zoning district and a built form overlay district.”
Source: 530.20.

Built form overlays (Chapter 540) regulate:

Maximum building height

Floor area ratio (FAR)

Lot coverage

Stepbacks

Upper-story setbacks

Minimum landscaped area

However, your parcel’s specific overlay (e.g., Interior 1 (BFI1), Interior 2 (BFI2), Corridor 6 (BFC6), Transit 10/15/etc.) is not identifiable from the zoning code; it requires the official zoning map.

Key Takeaways for Development in UN3

Intended scale: UN3 supports moderate-to-large-scale residential buildings, especially near transit.

Yard requirements: Governed by height-based tables (540-19 & 540-20).

Driveways & parking: Regulated by 555.510 and Table 555-5.

Lot sizes: Certain institutional or civic uses have minimum lot area requirements within UN districts.

Built form overlay: Required for all development; determines height and massing but must be obtained from the zoning map.

A developer-ready checklist for UN3

A side-by-side comparison of UN1 vs. UN2 vs. UN3

A parcel-specific built form analysis once you provide the built form overlay district from the zoning map.

question: summary zoning requirements for urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district, Minneapolis,  MN to build accessory dwelling unit (ADU) for single-family home.
response:
Below is a zoning-code-grounded summary of the requirements for constructing an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) on a single-family home in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district in Minneapolis.

Because ADUs are regulated citywide by Chapter 550, Article IX (Accessory Dwelling Unit Standards) and yard/setback rules in Chapter 540—not by differences between UN1/UN2/UN3—the requirements below apply to any ADU in UN2, unless your built form overlay district imposes additional limits (e.g., height, FAR, lot coverage). Built-form districts are required per §530.20 but were not included in retrieved text.

SUMMARY OF ADU REQUIREMENTS FOR A SINGLE-FAMILY HOME IN UN2
1. ADU Eligibility

An ADU is permitted only accessory to a principal single-family or two-family dwelling.

“Internal, attached, and detached accessory dwelling units shall be allowed accessory to a principal residential structure.”

Only one ADU is allowed per zoning lot.

“No more than one (1) accessory dwelling unit shall be allowed on a zoning lot.”

ADUs are prohibited accessory to all other uses.

“Accessory dwelling units shall be prohibited accessory to all other uses.”

2. Development Standards – All ADUs
a. Minimum dwelling unit size

Minimum GFA for any dwelling unit, including ADUs, is 300 sq ft.

b. Balcony & deck placement

Balconies may not face an interior side yard.

Decks must be ≥ 5 ft from interior side property lines.

3. Additional Rules by ADU Type
A. Internal ADUs (inside existing house)

Standards at §550.1440:

Max size: 800 sq ft, unless located in an existing portion of the house built before Jan 1, 2015; may not exceed first-floor area.

Must be located on one level.

No new street-facing entrances.

Exterior stairs must be enclosed or fully at the rear.

B. Attached ADUs (structurally attached)

§550.1450:

Max GFA: 800 sq ft.

No new street-facing entrances.

Exterior stairs to second levels must be at the rear or enclosed.

Exterior materials must match the principal structure.

C. Detached ADUs (separate backyard structure)

§550.1460 gives the most detailed rules:

Height

Max height: 21 ft (unless granted a variance).

Size limits

Two independent caps apply:

Total GFA cap:

≤ 1,300 sq ft OR 16% of lot area, whichever is greater.

Absolute maximum: ≤ 1,600 sq ft OR ≤ GFA of principal house.

Footprint cap:

Combined footprint of all detached accessory structures + drive/parking areas integrated with ADU ≤ 800 sq ft or 10% of lot area, whichever is greater.

Setbacks

Interior side yard: minimum 3 ft (unless garage door faces the side lot line—then no reduction allowed).

Rear yard: may be reduced to 3 ft (except where garage door faces rear lot line—then no reduction).

Distance to principal house: must be ≥ 10 ft.

Reverse corner lots: separate restrictions apply; see §550.1460(7).

Materials & Windows

Exterior materials must be durable: masonry, brick, stone, wood, cement-based siding, or glass.

At least 5% window area on facades facing alleys or public streets.

4. Yard & Setback Requirements in UN2 (Apply to Principal & Accessory Structures)

ADU setbacks must also respect Chapter 540 yard rules for Urban Neighborhood districts:

Interior side & rear yards (for any residential structure)

Based on building height (Table 540-20):

If < 42 ft → minimum 5 ft

If 42–52.99 ft → 7 ft

If 53–63.99 ft → 9 ft
… increasing thereafter

But ADUs have their own allowed reductions (3 ft) per §550.1460, which override general requirements.

Front yard (set by built form district)

Table 540-18:

Interior 1/2/3: 20 ft

Corridor 3/4: 15 ft

Transit overlays: 15 ft or 10 ft if fronting a goods & services corridor

ADUs behind principal structures usually do not affect front yard.

5. Minimum Lot Size

There is no minimum lot size requirement for ADUs.
ADU standards do not cite minimum lot area in §550.1430–1460.

6. Occupancy Rules

Only internal ADUs may trigger an owner-occupancy covenant, depending on building code classification.

Covenant requirement for internal ADUs is described in §550.1440 and §550.1450.

Detached ADUs do not require owner-occupancy under retrieved text.

7. Permit Process

ADUs undergo administrative review (§550.1410):

“The zoning administrator shall conduct the administrative review of all applications for an accessory dwelling unit.”

No Planning Commission review is needed unless variances are required.

SUMMARY (Concise)

For a single-family home in UN2, you can build:

Detached ADU

Max 21 ft height

Max 1,300–1,600 sq ft GFA depending on lot size

Min 3 ft side & rear setbacks (with exceptions)

Must be ≥ 10 ft from principal house

Footprint ≤ 800 sq ft or 10% lot area

Durable exterior materials; window minimums

Attached/Internal ADU

Max 800 sq ft

No new street-facing entrances

Exterior stairs restricted

Internal ADUs must be on one level

General Rules

Only one ADU per lot

Must be accessory to single-family or two-family dwelling

Minimum 300 sq ft floor area

Decks ≥ 5 ft from interior side lot line

Yard requirements vary by built form overlay district (not included in retrieved code)

If you want, I can next produce a parcel-specific ADU feasibility checklist once you provide your built form overlay district for the property.

-  The multifamily home has 5 dwellings.
- The  side and rear yard setback will be 5 feet all around.

Output: A list with each property specification and its evaluation based on the zoning ordinance, referencing specific parts of the code for each item.
response:
Below is a zoning-code–grounded evaluation of whether your proposed specifications meet the requirements for constructing a multi-family home in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district of Minneapolis.
All citations come directly from the ordinance text you provided.

⚠️ Important: You asked specifically about small multi-family home construction in UN2—not single-family or ADU construction. UN2 allows multi-family uses, but whether your specific proposal is allowed depends on dimensional standards and the built form overlay district, which the ordinance requires for all parcels.

✅ EVALUATION OF EACH PROPOSED SPECIFICATION
1. Lot Area: 1,000 sq ft
Evaluation: Cannot determine fully; very likely too small for 5-unit multifamily development.

Why:
The zoning code does not specify minimum lot size for multifamily buildings within the text you provided, but all development standards for multifamily buildings depend on the built form overlay district, per:

“All property… shall be located within a primary zoning district and a built form overlay district.”
§530.20

The built form district controls:

Floor area ratio (FAR)

Lot coverage

Building height

Maximum allowed building size

Minimum setbacks and massing

Without the built form overlay district, it is not possible to evaluate whether a 1,000-sq-ft lot can physically or legally contain a 5-unit building.

Most built form districts would not allow a 5-unit building on a 1,000-sq-ft lot.

Conclusion:

❓ Cannot determine; likely non-compliant without knowing built form overlay.

2. Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) Height: 20 feet
Evaluation: Allowed (if the ADU is detached); but irrelevant for multifamily.

If the ADU is a detached ADU, its height limit is:

“A detached accessory dwelling unit shall not exceed twenty-one (21) feet in height.”
§550.1460(1)

Thus, 20 ft is compliant.

However:

ADUs are accessory to single-family or two-family homes only.

ADUs must be accessory to “a single-family or two-family dwelling.”
§550.1420

You cannot attach or associate an ADU with a 5-unit multifamily building.

Conclusion:

✖ Non-compliant for a multifamily building (ADUs not permitted accessory to multifamily).
✔ Height is compliant only if ADU were allowed.

3. Number of dwellings: 5
Evaluation: Allowed as a use in UN2, but approval depends entirely on built form district constraints.

Urban Neighborhood (UN) districts permit multifamily residential uses, per:

Urban Neighborhood districts allow “small scale institutional and civic uses, and multi-family residential uses.”
§530.100

Thus, 5 dwelling units is a permitted use category.

However, whether you can build 5 units on your specific lot depends on:

Maximum FAR

Maximum height

Minimum setbacks

Lot coverage limits

Minimum open space

Required building massing

All determined by the built form overlay district.

On a 1,000 sq ft lot, most built form districts would not allow a 5-unit building due to FAR or coverage limits.

Conclusion:

✔ Use is allowed,
❓ but actual feasibility cannot be evaluated without built form overlay.

4. Side and Rear Yard Setbacks: 5 ft
Evaluation: Compliant for buildings under 42 ft tall.

Table 540-20 establishes required interior side and rear yards for residential structures:

If building height < 42 ft, required side and rear yards = 5 ft
Table 540-20

Thus, if your building height is < 42 ft, the 5-ft yard requirement is satisfied.

If the building is taller, setbacks increase to 7–9 ft and more.

Conclusion:

✔ Compliant if the building is under 42 ft.

ADU height 20 ft	✖ Not allowed with multifamily; height OK	ADUs only allowed accessory to single/two-family (§550.1420); height limit 21 ft (§550.1460)
5 dwellings	✔ Allowed use category in UN2	UN districts allow multifamily (§530.100)

Without this classification, dimensional compliance cannot be evaluated.

Use (multifamily) is allowed in UN2.

question:
Aim: I am trying to fill a permit application for constructing a small multi-family building in urban neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district,  Minneapolis, MN. Provide a checklist of all information and documentation I need to fill the permit application successfully per the zoning ordinance.

response:
Below is a zoning-code–grounded checklist of the information and documentation you must provide when applying for a permit to construct a small multi-family building in the Urban Neighborhood 2 (UN2) zoning district in Minneapolis.
This checklist includes all requirements contained in the zoning ordinance excerpts you provided, focusing on UN2, Urban Neighborhood district rules, and citywide built form + multifamily development standards.

Because Minneapolis requires every parcel to have both a primary zoning district (UN2) and a built form overlay district, items related to height, FAR, and front setbacks depend on the built form overlay.

✅ PERMIT APPLICATION CHECKLIST – SMALL MULTIFAMILY BUILDING (UN2)
1. Property Identification & Zoning Verification

Provide the following:

 Property address

 Parcel ID and legal description

 Primary zoning district: UN2

 Built Form Overlay District (required for height, FAR, coverage)

“All property… shall be located within a primary zoning district and a built form overlay district.”
§530.20

 Any additional overlay districts (Shoreland, MRCCA, Transit Priority, Historic, etc.)

 Existing survey showing lot boundaries and dimensions

2. Site Plan Requirements (Chapter 540 – Built Form Standards)

Submit a scaled site plan including:

Lot & Layout

 Lot area and lot width

 North arrow and scale

 Location of all existing and proposed structures

 Setbacks from all lot lines:

Interior side & rear yards (Table 540-20):

If building height < 42 ft → 5 ft minimum

If ≥ 42 ft → 7–9 ft depending on height

Front yard (Table 540-18):

Depends on Built Form Overlay (e.g., Interior districts require 20 ft)

Corner side yard (if applicable):

< 42 ft → 8 ft (Table 540-19)

Open area & access

 Outdoor open space (if required by built form)

 Trash/recycling enclosure location

 Mechanical equipment location (must be screened)

3. Building Design Documentation (Chapter 550)
A. Architectural plans

 Full set of architectural drawings

 Elevations showing building height, façade materials, windows

 Floor plans for each level and each dwelling unit

 Gross floor area calculations

 Floor area ratio (FAR) calculation

Required because FAR limits come from built form overlay

 Lot coverage calculation

B. Dwelling unit standards

 Each dwelling unit must be ≥ 300 sq ft

“Each dwelling unit shall contain not less than three hundred (300) square feet…”
§550.310

 Habitable width ≥ 18 ft for 80% of floor area
§550.310

C. Building entrance requirements

 Principal entrance faces the front lot line

“Dwellings shall include a principal entrance facing the front lot line.”
§550.320(a)

 Walkway to sidewalk ≥ 3 ft wide
§550.320(b)

4. Parking, Driveways, and Access (Chapter 555)
Driveways

 Driveway location and dimensions

“Driveways may be located in required front and side yards for residential uses…”
§555.510

Parking

 On-site parking spaces (if included) with dimensions

 Off-site parking agreements (if used), permitted in:
UN3, RM1–RM3, CM1–CM4, DT1–DT2
Table 555-5

Bicycle parking

 Indoor/outdoor bicycle parking (amount depends on # of units; not included in excerpt but required citywide)

5. Utilities, Stormwater, and Infrastructure

Provide plans for:

 Water and sanitary sewer connections

 Stormwater management plan (grading, drainage)

 Erosion control plan

 Gas/electrical/telecom utility connections

Per §520.70, essential infrastructure is allowed in all districts but must be shown on plans.
§520.70

6. Landscape & Screening Requirements

 Landscape plan showing trees, plantings, open space

 Screening of mechanicals, trash/recycling

 Fence locations and heights
(Required under citywide site plan standards)

7. Building Code & Regulatory Compliance

Include:

 Statement of compliance with Minnesota State Building Code

 Fire code compliance

 Energy code compliance

 Accessibility compliance (ADA for common areas)

 Environmental compliance (soil, drainage)

Required per general regulatory compliance section:

“Structures shall comply with all city, regional, state, and federal regulations.”
§520.60

8. Required Zoning Approvals
A. Site Plan Review

All new multifamily buildings require site plan review under Chapter 550 (Standards included in excerpt: entrances, unit sizes, access, etc.).

 Completed site plan review application

 Responses to site plan standards

B. Variances

Needed if you cannot meet:

Required setbacks

Height limits (from built form overlay)

Lot coverage

FAR

Entrance orientation rules

C. Building Permit

After zoning approval, submit:

Structural plans

Mechanical, electrical, plumbing (MEP) plans

9. Optional Supporting Documentation

 Land survey prepared by licensed surveyor (highly recommended)

 Shadow study (if close to lot lines)

 Construction management plan (noise, staging, access)

UN2 + built form overlay district

ADA

Energy code

Erosion control

Approval Steps
